_comment: Every path in a public skill repo belongs to exactly one class (Skill Repo Spec v1 s9).
TOOL    = code, SKILL.md, docs. Public, hand-written, contains no data.
FIXTURE = tests and examples. Public, but SYNTHETIC and produced by tools/make_fixtures.py.
DATA    = anything a real run produced. NEVER git-tracked; lives in the private companion
config, resolved at runtime by tools/datadir.py. The repo ships only the schema.
Enforced by tools/data_boundary.py, in .githooks and in CI.
_fixture_note: golden_classify.jsonl was the 2026-07 leak in THIS repo: it had been built by pasting real
emails out of the inbox the skill reads. It is now OUTPUT -- the case table in
tools/make_fixtures.py is the only input, and data_boundary.py requires byte-equality with a
fresh generator run. A real email cannot be regenerated, so pasting one back in fails loudly.
Never hand-edit the .jsonl. Edit CASES, run: python tools/make_fixtures.py
_data_note: Empty because this repo already points its output pipe OUTSIDE itself, and nothing DATA-class
is git-tracked (audited 2026-07-13). Every real-run artifact resolves to:
~/.email-monitor-config/registry.json   accounts, VIP senders, thresholds, cred pointers
The registry SCHEMA ships as a GENERATOR (scripts/init_config.py, REGISTRY), not a static
*.example: init_config.py writes it INTO the private dir. So the public repo stays an
uninitialized tool, and there is no *.example here to drift out of sync with the real shape.
_data_sealed_note: These are the in-repo paths where real data would land if the skill were ever pointed at itself.
registry.json is the richest target in this skill: real accounts, real VIP senders, cred paths.
_personal_layer.json is VIP senders, which ARE PII (CONFIG.md).
Verified 2026-07-13: none has ever been tracked, in any commit, on any ref. So this is a FORWARD
seal, not a purge -- nothing was cleaned up here, and the class is declared to keep it that way.
They are all gitignored already, but .gitignore is ADVISORY: `git add -f` walks straight through
it, and an agent trying to make a freshly cloned skill 'work out of the box' will reach for
exactly that. This makes the seal enforceable, in pre-commit, pre-push and CI.
Sealed paths are exempt from the *.example rule: a path the tool must never use is not owed a
schema, and publishing one would advertise it.
